title: audit — realtime broadcast channels
description: Two broadcast channels for live compliance-dashboard updates. Tenant-level retention-policy channel + audit-export channel. Audit rows themselves are NOT broadcast — they are potentially high-volume + PII-laden, and there is no operational need for live audit tail (compliance dashboards refresh on demand). Reverb driver is the default transport.

channels.name: tenant.{tenantId}.audit.retention-policy
channels.description: Retention-policy lifecycle updates. Powers the compliance-officer dashboard's retention-policy widget with live status transitions.
channels.authorization: sanctum::authed AND tenant_matches AND (compliance_officer OR owner role)
channels.notes: override_reason is broadcast in full to the compliance-officer role. Payload is trimmed for owner role (reason omitted; only the fact-of-change + effective retention_years).

channels.name: tenant.{tenantId}.audit.exports
channels.description: Audit export lifecycle updates. Powers the compliance-officer dashboard's active-exports widget + a live progress indicator on running exports.
channels.authorization: sanctum::authed AND tenant_matches AND (compliance_officer OR owner role)
events: AuditExportProgress (internal — informational chunks during a long-running export)
channels.notes: signed_url is NEVER broadcast on this channel (it's a bearer token). The channel emits only the export status; the URL is delivered via AuditExportReadyNotification (in-app + email), which is a targeted 1-to-1 delivery, not a broadcast.

explicitly_not_broadcast.reason: Audit rows are potentially high-volume + PII-laden in old_values/new_values. Broadcasting them live would (a) explode channel bandwidth for high-mutation tenants + (b) risk PII leakage over the wire. Compliance dashboards refresh on demand + poll /api/v1/audits when a user is looking at the surface.
Purge is nightly + not user-facing in real-time. The weekly digest notification is the right cadence.
